title: A bushing contact and contact system
abstract: The invention relates to a bushing contact and a contact system with such a bushing contact, wherein the bushing contact has a contact housing and a contact element, wherein the contact element is arranged in the contact housing and is connected to the contact housing, wherein the contact housing has a first side wall and a second side wall, wherein the first side wall is arranged offset from the second side wall, wherein the first side wall and the second side wall delimit a housing interior, wherein the contact element is arranged in the housing interior, wherein the contact housing has an aperture, wherein the contact element has a continuous, flexible strip made of an electrically conductive material, wherein the contact element is connected to the first side wall by a first end and to the second side wall by a second end, wherein the contact element is supported on the first side wall in a first strip section and on the second side wall in a second strip section, wherein a third strip section of the contact element is arranged opposite the aperture, wherein the third strip section is formed to be applied to the plug contact in a flexible manner when a plug contact is introduced through the aperture into the housing interior.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A bushing contact (15), -having a contact housing (50) and a contact element (55) , - wherein the contact element (55) is arranged in the contact housing (50) and is connected to the contact housing (50) , - wherein the contact housing (50) has a first side wall (60) and a second side wall (65), -wherein the first side wall (60) is arranged offset from the second side wall (65), -wherein the first side wall (60) and the second side wall (65) delimit a housing interior (80), - wherein the contact element (55) is arranged in the housing interior (80), -wherein the contact housing (50) has an aperture (110) , - characterised in that - the contact element (55) has a continuous, flexible strip (130) made of an electrically conductive material , - wherein the contact element (55) is supported on the first side wall (60) in a first strip section (165) and on the second side wall (65) in a second strip section (170) , -wherein a third strip section (175) of the contact element (55) is arranged opposite the aperture (110), -wherein the third strip section (175) is formed to be applied to the plug contact (20) in a flexible manner 

 when a plug contact (20) is introduced through the aperture (110) into the housing interior (80) . 2. The bushing contact according to Claim 1, wherein the contact element (55) is connected to the first side wall (60) by a first end (135) and to the second side wall (65) by a second end (140) . 3. The bushing contact (15) according to Claim 1 or 2, -wherein the third strip section (175) has a first limb (180), a second limb (185) and a first arcuate region (190) , -wherein the first arcuate region (190) is arranged between the first limb (180) and the second limb (185), -wherein the first limb (180) is arranged offset from the second limb (185), - wherein the first arcuate region (190) is arranged opposite the aperture (110). 4. The bushing contact (15) according to Claim 3, - wherein on a side which faces away from the aperture (110), the first limb (180) is connected to the first arcuate region (190) and the second limb (185) is connected to the first arcuate region (190), -wherein on a side which faces the aperture (110), the first limb (180) is coupled to the first strip section (165) and the second limb (185) is coupled to the second strip section (170). 5. The bushing contact (15) according to Claim 3 or 4, -wherein the first arcuate region (190) encloses an 

 angle which is greater than 180 degrees and smaller than 240 degrees and/or is formed to be C-shaped. 6. The bushing contact (15) according to any one of Claims 3 to 5 , - wherein the contact element (55) has a second arcuate region (195) between the first limb (180) and the first strip section (165), -wherein the first arcuate region (190) has a first curvature and the second arcuate region (195) has a second curvature, - wherein the second curvature is in the opposite direction to the first curvature. 7. The bushing contact (15) according to Claim 6, -wherein the contact element (55) has a third arcuate region (200) between the second limb (185) and the second strip section (170), -wherein the third arcuate region (200) has a third curvature, - wherein the second curvature and the third curvature run in the same direction. 8. The bushing contact (15) according to any one of Claims 3 to 7, -wherein the first strip section (165) and the first limb (180) are arranged substantially parallel to each other, -and/or wherein the second strip section (170) and the second limb (185) are arranged substantially parallel to each other, 

 - and/or -wherein the first strip section (165) is arranged parallel to the second strip section (170) . 9. The bushing contact (15) according to any one of Claims 3 to 8, -wherein the first limb (180) has a first convex arch (285) to the second limb (185) . 10. The bushing contact (15) according to Claim 9, -wherein the second limb (185) has a second convex arch (290) to the first limb (180), -wherein the first arch (285) and the second arch (290) are selected such that there is a minimal distance (b) between the first limb (180) and the second limb (185) substantially centrally in the transverse direction. 11. The bushing contact (15) according to any one of Claims 3 to 10, -wherein at the first arcuate region (190) at least one first bulge (245) is arranged on a side which faces the aperture (110) . 12. The bushing contact (15) according to Claim 11, -wherein at the first arcuate region (190) at least one further bulge (265) is arranged on a side which faces the aperture (110), -wherein the bulge (245) and the further bulge (265) are arranged in one plane, - wherein the plane is arranged parallel to a bending axis (220) of the first arcuate region (190) . 13. A contact system (10) -with a bushing contact (15) and a plug contact (20), -wherein the bushing contact (15) is formed according to any one of the preceding claims, -wherein the plug contact (20) engages through the aperture (110) , - wherein the third strip section (175) bears against the plug contact (20) at least in sections. 14. The contact system (10) according to Claim 13, -wherein the third strip section (175) is formed such that the contour of the third strip section (175) adapts following an outer contour of the plug contact (20) . 15. The contact system (10) according to Claim 13 or 14, -wherein the second arcuate region (195) is formed such that when the plug contact (20) is plugged into the contact element (55), the strip (130) rolls on a surface (30, 35) of the plug contact (20) and the first limb (180) is applied to the surface (30, 35) of the plug contact (20) .
